title: 10 best herbal capsules for nausea

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with nausea because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes. These compounds have been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, soothing irritated stomach lining and reducing inflammation. As a result, maidenhair tree capsules can help alleviate nausea symptoms by promoting a sense of calmness and balance in the body, making them a natural and effective remedy for managing nausea-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: These antioxidants have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nausea by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviating symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
Terpenoids: These compounds have been shown to inhibit the release of serotonin, a hormone that can cause nausea and vomiting, helping to alleviate morning sickness and other forms of nausea.
Ginkgolides: These unique compounds have anti-spasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce cramping and spasms that can contribute to nausea.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation and modulating neurotransmitter activity in the brain.

Bilobalide: A sesquiterpene lactone present in Ginkgo biloba, bilobalide has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce nausea by blocking the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Quercetin: A type of flavonoid, quercetin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate nausea by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate nausea.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds contain a compound called ginkgotoxin, which has been shown to have anti-emetic properties, helping to prevent nausea.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain flavonoids and terpenoids, similar to the leaves, which may help reduce nausea and alleviate other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500mg of ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the ginkgo biloba powder with 100mg of cellulose powder and 50mg of silica powder in a bowl.
Add 20ml of glycerin and 20ml of purified water to the powder mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Use a capsule maker to fill 1000mg capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly.
Label the capsules with the date of manufacture and the contents using a permanent marker.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap capsules helps with nausea because they contain Baicalin, a flavonoid that has been traditionally used to calm digestive issues. When taken in capsule form, skullcap is absorbed into the bloodstream and helps to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing inflammation and soothing the discomfort associated with nausea. Additionally, skullcap's calming effects on the nervous system can also help alleviate feelings of anxiety or stress that may be exacerbating nausea.
remedy_properties: Scutellarein: A flavonoid compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.

Baicalin: A flavonoid glycoside that has been found to have anti-emetic properties, meaning it helps to reduce the occurrence of vomiting and nausea by blocking the action of neurotransmitters that stimulate emesis (vomiting).
Caffeic acid: A phenolic acid that has been demonstrated to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce nausea by reducing pain and inflammation in the digestive system.
remedy_constituents: Baicalein: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, helping to reduce nausea by alleviating digestive inflammation and oxidative stress.
Baicalin: This flavonoid glycoside has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm nausea-inducing gastrointestinal disturbances and promote a sense of well-being.
Wogonin: This flavone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce nausea by protecting the stomach lining from damage and promoting a healthy digestive environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Scutellaria baicalensis are widely used due to their high concentration of baicalein and baicalin, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also commonly used, as they contain similar bioactive compounds to the roots, such as baicalein and baicalin, which help alleviate nausea.
(optional) Stems: The stems may also be used to some extent, but it is less common than using roots or leaves, as they contain lower concentrations of bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of scutellaria baicalensis roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer at 50-60°c for 2-3 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mill or grinder to increase bioavailability.
Mix the powder with 1-2% starch or cellulose as filler and 0.5-1% magnesium stearate as lubricant in a 1:10 ratio.
Fill the powder mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine to produce 500-1000 capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with nausea because they contain a natural ingredient called Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower. This herb has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and alleviate symptoms of anxiety, which can often contribute to feelings of queasiness. The capsules work by gently soothing the digestive tract and reducing inflammation, allowing for smoother digestion and absorption of nutrients. As a result, Maypop capsules can provide quick relief from nausea and help restore balance to the body.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Maypop capsules contain a compound called flavonoids, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms that can contribute to nausea.

Anti-inflammatory property: Maypop contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linoleic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate nausea caused by gastrointestinal irritation.
Sedative and anxiolytic property: The flavonoids and other bioactive compounds in Maypop have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress that can exacerbate nausea.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: An alkaloid found in Passiflora incarnata, harmane has been shown to have antiemetic properties by reducing the activity of the central nervous system, thereby alleviating nausea.

Isovitexin: A flavonoid glycoside in Passiflora incarnata, isovitexin has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its ability to reduce nausea by mitigating underlying inflammation and oxidative stress.

Flavonoids (specifically flavonols): The flavonols present in Passiflora incarnata, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been reported to exhibit antiemetic activity by modulating the neurotransmitter system and reducing the excitability of the gastrointestinal tract, leading to a decrease in nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help in reducing nausea.
Fruits: The fruits are widely used as they contain high amounts of passifloric acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate nausea.
Roots: The roots are often used due to their rich content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat nausea and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_recipe: Dry 250g of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in a dehydrator at 40°c for 6 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 3 minutes.
Mix 250g of the powdered passiflora incarnata with 250g of vegetable cellulose powder in a large bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the passiflora incarnata mixture and seal them immediately.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blackhaw capsules helps with nausea because they contain extracts of the Viburnum prunifolium plant, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system. The capsule's active compounds work to calm the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea. Additionally, blackhaw capsules may help regulate digestion and absorption, further alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting. As a result, herbal blackhaw capsules offer a natural and gentle way to alleviate nausea and restore overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Blackhaw capsules may help alleviate nausea by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation, which can reduce feelings of dizziness and discomfort.

Antispasmodic properties: The active compounds in blackhaw capsules, such as scopoletin and berberine, have been shown to relax smooth muscle tissue and reduce spasms in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with gastrointestinal disorders.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Blackhaw capsules may also exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation that can contribute to nausea and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Viburnic acid: Viburnic acid is an iridoid glycoside that has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce nausea by minimizing inflammation in the stomach and intestines.

Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid is a triterpenoid saponin that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help alleviate nausea by protecting the stomach lining from damage and reducing oxidative stress.

Viburnin: Viburnin is an iridoid glycoside that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help reduce nausea by relaxing the stomach muscles and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Viburnum prunifolium are used due to their antiemetic properties, which help alleviate nausea.
Seeds: Viburnum prunifolium seeds are used in herbal supplements to treat nausea due to their emetic and antiemetic properties.
Fruits: The fruits of Viburnum prunifolium, also known as blue viburnum, are used to make herbal supplements that help alleviate nausea and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_recipe: Dry the viburnum prunifolium leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried viburnum prunifolium leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the ground powder with 2 parts of a natural carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a ratio.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the mixture and seal them securely to preserve freshness.
Store the herbal viburnum prunifolium capsules in a cool dark place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava capsules helps with nausea because they contain a unique blend of natural compounds that work together to calm the stomach and alleviate discomfort. The herbal extract in kava capsules has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, including nausea, vomiting, and bloating. Its sedative properties help to reduce inflammation and relax muscle contractions, allowing the body to regain its natural balance and eliminating feelings of queasiness. This natural remedy provides fast relief from nausea, making it an effective solution for those struggling with morning sickness or motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: Kava's primary active compounds, kavalactones, have anti-nausea and anti-emetic effects by interacting with neurotransmitters in the brain to reduce anxiety and discomfort associated with nausea.

Antispasmodic properties: Herbal kava capsules have antispasmodic properties that help relax smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to nausea and vomiting.

GABA-mimetic activity: Kavalactones in herbal kava capsules also exhibit GABA-mimetic activity, meaning they bind to GABA receptors, which helps regulate the nervous system's response to stress and anxiety, ultimately reducing feelings of nausea.
remedy_constituents: Kavalactones: These lactones act as antispasmodics, which help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce nausea-inducing spasms.
Isopteropodine: This alkaloid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea associated with inflammation.
Kavain: This kavalactone has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety-related nausea and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in kavalactones and other bioactive compounds, they help alleviate nausea and other gastrointestinal issues.
Barks: Containing some of the same bioactive compounds as the rhizomes, the barks are used to make capsules for nausea relief, although to a lesser extent.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 500 grams of dried root bark of piper methysticum under full moon.
Dry the harvested material in a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind 250 grams of dried root bark into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix the ground powder with 250 grams of vegetable cellulose powder and 50 grams of magnesium stearate.
Fill 100-count capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with nausea because of its natural ability to soothe the digestive system. The herb contains compounds that work as a natural anti-inflammatory, reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines that can cause nausea and discomfort. Additionally, valerian root has been shown to stimulate digestion, helping to move food through the body more efficiently and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating. By promoting relaxation and reducing digestive discomfort, herbal valerian capsules provide a natural remedy for managing nausea.
remedy_properties: GABA-like effects: Valerian root contains compounds that interact with GABA receptors in the brain, producing a calming effect that can help alleviate nausea by reducing anxiety and stress.

Antispasmodic properties: Valerian's antispasmodic compounds help to relax muscle contractions in the stomach and intestines, which can reduce nausea and vomiting caused by digestive issues or other conditions.

Digestive soothing: Valerian root has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort that may contribute to nausea.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic Acid: This sesquiterpene acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which helps to reduce nausea by modulating the activity of the brain's neurotransmitters involved in nausea regulation.

Valerenal: This sesquiterpene also acts as a GABA receptor agonist, similar to valerenic acid, and contributes to the anti-nausea effects of Valeriana officinalis by influencing the brain's nausea response.

Acidic Phenylethanoid Glycosides (e.g., valeranose): These compounds have been shown to have a sedative effect, which can help alleviate nausea by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, two common triggers of nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate nausea and reduce stress.
Flowers: Used for their mild sedative and anti-anxiety effects, which can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried valeriana officinalis root, grind it into a fine powder.
Mix 250g of the valeriana powder with 250g of vegetable cellulose in a bowl.
Add 5g of silica gel as an anti-caking agent to the powder mixture.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with nausea because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This compound can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and digestive tract, which can contribute to feelings of nausea and discomfort. Additionally, licorice root has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues such as heartburn, indigestion, and bloating, making it a natural remedy for alleviating nausea and promoting overall digestive health. By reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive system, herbal licorice capsules can provide fast and effective relief from nausea.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting.
Flavonoids: Licorice root contains flavonoids, such as licochalcone A, which have been shown to inhibit enzymes that contribute to nausea and vomiting, providing relief from digestive discomfort.
Saponins: Saponins, like glabridin, in licorice root can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, alleviating nausea and vomiting associated with digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin that helps alleviate nausea by inhibiting the vomiting center in the brain and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.

Flavonoids (specifically, isoorientin and isovitalin): A type of phenolic compound that helps alleviate nausea by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea-inducing inflammation.

Licoflavonoid (a glycoside of flavonoids): A compound that helps alleviate nausea by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizic acid is extracted from the roots to make capsules for nausea due to its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the digestive system.
Roots: Licorice extract, also from the roots, is used to make capsules for nausea as it helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of nausea and stomach upset.
Roots: Glycyrrhetic acid, another compound found in the roots, is used to make capsules for nausea as it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a yield of 25 percent.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 10 grams of starch and 10 grams of cellulose powder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powder mixture and seal immediately.
Store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with nausea because they contain an essential oil called anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This oil works to soothe the digestive system and calm the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort that can cause nausea. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to expel gas and reduce bloating, further alleviating nausea symptoms. By addressing the underlying causes of nausea, fennel capsules can provide quick and effective relief for those suffering from this uncomfortable condition.
remedy_properties: Carminative effects: Fennel capsules have been shown to reduce digestive spasms and relax the muscles in the stomach, which helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting by releasing trapped gas.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in fennel capsules, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to nausea and other digestive issues.

Soothing effects on the digestive tract: Fennel's mucilage, a thick, protective coating that forms when it comes into contact with water, helps to soothe and protect the lining of the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with nausea.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole is a terpene that helps with nausea by acting as a natural antispasmodic and relaxing the muscles in the stomach, thereby reducing nausea and vomiting.
Foeniculin: Foeniculin is a sesquiterpene that helps with nausea by having a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation, and alleviating stomach cramps.
Apiol: Apiol is a phenolic compound that helps with nausea by stimulating digestion, relieving flatulence, and reducing spasms in the digestive tract, which in turn alleviates nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are commonly used to make capsules for nausea due to their high concentration of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties.
Leaves: Leaves are often used to make capsules for nausea as they contain a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that may help alleviate digestive issues.

Stems: The stems of Foeniculum vulgare are occasionally used to make capsules for nausea, possibly due to their ability to absorb and retain the medicinal compounds from the plant, making them a useful supplement.
remedy_recipe: Dry the foeniculum vulgare leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground foeniculum vulgare powder with a binding agent such as gelatin in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the foeniculum vulgare powder mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Seal the capsules and store them in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with nausea because they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety-related symptoms. Additionally, chamomile's soothing properties can calm the digestive system, easing stomach cramps and spasms that often accompany nausea. As a natural anti-inflammatory, chamomile may also help reduce irritation and inflammation in the gut, further alleviating nausea and vomiting symptoms. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe the stomach and promote digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate nausea symptoms.
GABA-mimetic activity: Chamomile's apigenin compound has been shown to interact with GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which can contribute to nausea relief.

Soil-based antioxidants: Chamomile capsules often contain soil-based organisms that produce antioxidant compounds, such as glutathione, which can help protect the stomach lining from oxidative stress and reduce nausea symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, helping to soothe the digestive system and alleviate nausea.
Chamazulene: A sesquiterpene that has anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which helps to calm the stomach muscles and reduce nausea.
Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene alcohol that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which helps to soothe the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate nausea.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make capsules for nausea due to their high content of apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties.
Seeds: They are used to make capsules for nausea due to their ability to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea and vomiting.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for nausea due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help relieve nausea and abdominal cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers at 10 am on a sunny day when flowers are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours using a food dehydrator.
Grind 30 grams of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 50 grams of gelatin in a 1:2 ratio and add 10 ml of distilled water.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and produce 100 capsules in 10 minutes using a standard 0.5 ml capacity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date capsules helps with nausea because they contain a unique blend of herbs that have been used for centuries in traditional Chinese medicine to calm the stomach and alleviate digestive discomfort. The natural ingredients in these capsules, such as licorice root and ginseng, work together to soothe the gastrointestinal tract and reduce inflammation, thereby minimizing symptoms of nausea and vomiting. As a result, individuals can experience relief from morning sickness, travel-induced queasiness, and other forms of nausea-related distress.
remedy_properties: Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the digestive system, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting symptoms.
Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in these capsules have been found to have anti-emetic properties, helping to reduce the frequency and severity of nausea and vomiting episodes.
Amino Acids and Vitamins: These capsules contain a range of essential amino acids and vitamins, including vitamin B6, which can help to calm the stomach, reduce inflammation, and alleviate nausea.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Ziziphus jujuba capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ziziphus jujuba, particularly quercetin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help stabilize the digestive system and alleviate nausea.
Glycosides: Glycosides in Ziziphus jujuba, such as jujuboside, have anti-emetic properties that help suppress nausea and vomiting by interacting with the brain's nausea centers.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used to make capsules due to their anthraquinones content, which has anti-emetic properties.
Seeds: They are used to make capsules as they contain saponins and other compounds that help reduce nausea and vomiting.
Barks: They are used to make capsules due to their flavonoids and alkaloids content, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried ziziphus jujuba fruits and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush.
Grind 250 grams of the cleaned ziziphus jujuba fruits into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 100 grams of the powder with 50 grams of gum arabic to form a uniform paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the paste and seal them tightly using a capsule sealer.
Label the capsules with the date of preparation and store them in a cool dry place for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with nausea because they contain extracts of Bacopa monnieri, a natural herb that has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for centuries. The herb is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe the digestive system and reduce nausea. Brahmi capsules may also help regulate digestion, absorb nutrients properly, and calm the nervous system, all of which can contribute to alleviating nausea symptoms. Additionally, Brahmi's adaptogenic properties can help the body adapt to stress, further reducing the likelihood of nausea.
remedy_properties: Stress-relieving: Brahmi's adaptogenic properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to nausea and other digestive issues.
Anti-inflammatory: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory compounds may help soothe and calm digestive tract inflammation, reducing discomfort and symptoms of nausea.
Antioxidant-rich: Brahmi contains a wealth of antioxidants that may help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, potentially reducing nausea and other symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in soothing digestive issues like nausea.
Buds: Containing high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of nausea.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried bacopa monnieri herbs into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 300mg of the ground herb with 50mg of glycerin and 20mg of silicon dioxide in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal tightly to prevent leakage.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.
Take one capsule orally twice daily, 30 minutes before meals or as needed to alleviate nausea symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marijuana capsules helps with nausea because they contain cannabidiol (CBD), a non-psychoactive compound found in cannabis, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. CBD interacts with the body's endocannabinoid system, specifically with receptors in the brain and gut, to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort. This relief from inflammation can help alleviate nausea and vomiting symptoms associated with conditions such as chemotherapy, Crohn's disease, and morning sickness during pregnancy. By soothing the digestive tract and reducing inflammation, herbal marijuana capsules can provide effective relief from nausea.
remedy_properties: Cannabinoids: The psychoactive compounds found in marijuana, such as THC and CBD, have anti-emetic effects, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting by binding to receptors in the brain.

Terpenes: The aromatic compounds found in marijuana, such as myrcene, limonene, and pinene, have been shown to have a positive impact on nausea relief by interacting with cannabinoids and other neurotransmitters.

Non-psychoactive compounds: Herbal marijuana capsules often contain non-psychoactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help alleviate nausea through their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain cannabidiolic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate nausea symptoms.
Stems: The stems are rich in cannabigerol, a non-psychoactive compound that has been found to have anti-emetic properties, making them effective for reducing nausea.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 10 grams of dried cannabis sativa leaves and flowers.
Combine the measured cannabis with 20 grams of coconut oil in a glass jar.
Place the mixture in a warm water bath for 4 hours to decarboxylate the cannabis.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the infused oil and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai capsules helps with nausea because they contain the root of the Dong quai plant, which has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to alleviate symptoms of morning sickness and digestive discomfort. The capsules work by stimulating blood flow and regulating body temperature, helping to calm the stomach and reduce feelings of queasiness. Additionally, dong quai is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and promote overall wellness. By taking dong quai capsules regularly, individuals may experience a significant reduction in nausea symptoms.
remedy_properties: Dried Rhizome Extract: Dong Quai's dried rhizome extract has anti-emetic properties, which help to alleviate nausea by reducing stomach contractions and increasing gastric motility.

Coumarin: Coumarin, a compound found in Dong Quai, has been shown to have a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which can help to alleviate nausea by reducing anxiety and stress-related symptoms.
Volatile Oils: Dong Quai contains volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Dong Quai are rich in saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea, due to their bitter and warming properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried angelica sinensis roots from a trusted supplier weighing approximately 500 grams.
Sort and clean the angelica sinensis roots removing any debris or contaminants by hand.
Grind the cleaned angelica sinensis roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Sift the powdered angelica sinensis through a 100-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles taking approximately 10 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with approximately 250 milligrams of sifted angelica sinensis powder and seal immediately for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with nausea because they contain bioactive compounds that have a natural anti-inflammatory effect on the digestive system. The plant's bitter compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpene lactones, stimulate digestion and alleviate bloating and discomfort. Additionally, dandelion's ability to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation helps to calm nausea and vomiting associated with digestive issues like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or menstrual cramps. This natural remedy provides relief from nausea without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Choleretic property: Dandelion capsules can increase bile production, which helps to alleviate nausea by relieving digestive discomfort and promoting healthy digestion.
Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids present in dandelion root may help reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, reducing nausea caused by irritation or infection.
Laxative property: Dandelion root is a natural gentle laxative, which can help alleviate nausea associated with constipation, as it promotes regular bowel movements and relieves bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are a natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic, making them effective in soothing digestive issues like nausea.
Seeds: The seeds contain mucilage, a natural thickening agent that can help calm stomach irritation and reduce nausea symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers or dried herb, ensuring quality control measures are in place.
Chop the harvested taraxacum officinale into small pieces to facilitate efficient extraction and preparation.
Combine chopped taraxacum officinale with 2 cups of boiling water, allowing it to steep for 30 minutes to create a potent infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter, collecting the liquid extract in a clean glass container for future use.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 500mg of the prepared taraxacum officinale extract, ensuring accurate dosing and optimal potency preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony capsules helps with nausea because they contain Baishao, a key ingredient that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to calm the stomach and relieve digestive discomfort. The capsules work by soothing the lining of the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation, and promoting the elimination of toxins from the body. This natural remedy also has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with various conditions such as pregnancy, chemotherapy, and motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chinese Peony Capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate nausea caused by inflammation and irritation in the digestive system.
Stomachic and carminative property: These capsules have a soothing effect on the stomach and intestines, calming nausea and reducing spasms and discomfort in the abdominal region.
Antispasmodic property: Chinese Peony Capsules can help relax smooth muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramps that often accompany nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Chinese Peony plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Chinese Peony plant have a calming effect on the stomach and can help alleviate symptoms of nausea, making them a popular ingredient in herbal remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh paeonia lactiflora flowers and dry them in shade for 7-10 days.
Weigh 20 grams of dried paeonia lactiflora flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered herb with 100 milliliters of pure glycerin and store the mixture at room temperature for 24 hours to create a tincture.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter and measure out 1 gram of the liquid extract per capsule using an eyedropper.
Fill gelatin capsules with 1 gram each of the paeonia lactiflora tincture and seal the capsules tightly to prepare for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with nausea because they contain silymarin, a bioflavonoid compound that has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This unique combination can help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that may be contributing to nausea and discomfort. Additionally, milk thistle's natural antispasmodic effects can help ease muscle contractions in the stomach and intestines, further alleviating symptoms of nausea.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in milk thistle, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been found to have a soothing effect on the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and indigestion.

Silychristin: Silychristin, another active compound in milk thistle, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other digestive disorders.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Milk thistle seeds contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex that has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing nausea symptoms.
Roots: The roots of the milk thistle plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea, due to their ability to stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried silybum marianum seeds in large quantities ensuring precise measurement for later use.
Grind the collected seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine one part powdered silybum marianum seed with two parts gelatin in a mixing bowl and let it sit for 30 minutes to hydrate.
Fill empty capsules shells with the resulting mixture and seal them tightly to preserve potency and freshness.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or in the freezer for extended use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with nausea because its active ingredients, including hyperforin and hypericin, have natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe the digestive tract. The herbs calm irritated stomach muscles, reducing inflammation and discomfort that can cause queasiness. Additionally, St John's Wort's antimicrobial properties may help eliminate underlying infections or imbalances in the gut microbiome, further contributing to its effectiveness in alleviating nausea symptoms.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate nausea caused by digestive issues.
Hypotensive effect: St John's Wort capsules have been shown to lower blood pressure, which can help to reduce feelings of lightheadedness and dizziness that often accompany nausea.

Serotonin modulation: St John's Wort has been found to affect serotonin levels in the body, which is a neurotransmitter involved in regulating nausea and vomiting. By modulating serotonin levels, St John's Wort may help to alleviate nausea caused by hormonal imbalances or other factors.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of hyperforin, the root has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in relieving nausea symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, St. John's Wort flowers have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality st john's wort flowers hypericum perforatum at peak blooming stage for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to preserve medicinal properties.
Crush dried st john's wort flowers into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered st john's wort with silica gel or calcium carbonate as filler materials in a ratio of 1:2 and capsule filling equipment.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared herbal mixture at a dosage of 250-500mg per capsule and store in airtight containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog holly capsules helps with nausea because they contain natural ingredients that work to soothe the digestive system. The herbal extract has a calming effect on the stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to queasiness. Additionally, the capsules help to regulate digestion, preventing food from stagnating in the stomach and alleviating symptoms of indigestion. As a result, dog holly capsules provide effective relief for dogs experiencing nausea due to various causes, such as motion sickness or food sensitivities.
remedy_properties: Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids have been shown to have a positive effect on gut motility, helping to regulate bowel movements and alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by gastrointestinal issues.

Alkaloids: Certain alkaloids present in dog holly capsules have been found to have anticholinergic properties, which can help to reduce the release of neurotransmitters that trigger nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain berberine, an alkaloid that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to calm the stomach, making it effective in alleviating nausea.

Barks: The barks of dog holly contain a compound called isoquinoline alkaloids, which have been shown to have antispasmodic effects, helping to relieve muscle cramps and spasms associated with nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ruscus aculeatus leaves measure 25 grams for the batch.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dry place for two hours.
Grind dried ruscus aculeatus leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix one teaspoon of the powdered herb with 500ml distilled water to create a decoction steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction and encapsulate the liquid using gelatin capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with nausea because of its active compound curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When taken as a supplement, curcumin can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can further aid in reducing nausea. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective way to combat nausea and restore digestive balance.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: These active compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation in the stomach lining and promoting healing.
Volatile oils: Turmeric's volatile oils, such as cineole and borneol, have been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to reduce symptoms of nausea and vomiting.

Piperine: The black pepper extract present in turmeric capsules (known as piperine) enhances bioavailability and absorption of curcuminoids, allowing them to reach the bloodstream more efficiently and increasing their anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are the thick, underground stems that contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, making them an ideal choice for extracting active compounds for medicinal use in nausea relief.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality turmeric roots and fill a coffee grinder with 250 grams of dried powder for optimal potency.
Measure out 1000 milligrams of the ground turmeric and mix it with 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a small bowl for enhanced absorption.
Heat the mixture over low heat in a double boiler for 10 minutes to allow the turmeric's active compounds to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding any remaining solids and preserving the liquid gold.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the infused oil and store them in an airtight container for up to six months or freeze for longer preservation.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for nausea are natural supplements that help alleviate nausea and its associated symptoms by targeting the underlying causes of discomfort. The best herbal capsules for nausea include Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Foeniculum vulgare, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinchona officinalis, Piper nigrum, Mentha x piperita, Avena sativa, Silybum marianum, and Cinnamomum verum. If left untreated, nausea can lead to dehydration, fatigue, and a decrease in appetite, ultimately affecting daily life, work productivity, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nausea that are treated with herbal capsules are digestive disorders such as bloating, indigestion, and stomach cramps. Other conditions like menstrual cramps, migraines, and stress-induced anxiety can also be alleviated using herbal remedies in capsule form. Additionally, certain herbs may help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with nausea is a blend of Ginger, Peppermint, and Fennel. Ginger capsules contain gingerols and shogaols, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that aid digestion and reduce nausea. Peppermint capsules calm the stomach and alleviate nausea through their soothing effects on the digestive system. Fennel capsules, rich in anethole, help to ease digestive discomfort and bloating. Together, these herbs work synergistically to provide fast and effective relief from nausea.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Obstetrics and Gynecology, ginger capsules were found to be effective in relieving nausea by significantly reducing the severity of nausea in pregnant women. The study, which involved 70 women with nausea and vomiting of pregnancy, found that the ginger group had a decrease in visual analog scores of post-therapy minus baseline nausea by 2.1 +/- 1.9, compared to the placebo group's decrease of 0.9 +/- 2.2 (P =.014). Furthermore, the number of vomiting episodes also decreased significantly in the ginger group (1.4 +/- 1.3) compared to the placebo group (0.3 +/- 1.1, P <.001), indicating that herbal capsules containing ginger can be a good option for alleviating nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with nausea because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken, gingerol works to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach, leading to a decrease in nausea symptoms. Additionally, gingerol has been shown to slow down gastric emptying, allowing the body to properly digest food and absorb nutrients. This can provide quick relief from nausea and vomiting, making it a popular natural remedy for nausea and digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which help to relax the stomach muscles and reduce inflammation that may be contributing to nausea.

Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic effects, but with an additional ability to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, further alleviating nausea.

Volatile oils (particularly Zingiberene and Bisabolol): These terpenes have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce nausea by alleviating inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach and intestines, promoting a more comfortable digestive environment.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale for nausea-relieving capsules, as they contain the highest concentration of active compounds like gingerols and shogaols.

Stems: Some sources suggest that the stems of Zingiber officinale may be used to make capsules for nausea, possibly due to their ability to absorb and concentrate the active compounds found in the plant.

Roots: Although not as commonly used as the rhyzomes, the roots of Zingiber officinale may also be utilized to make capsules for nausea relief, as they contain some of the plant's bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes for their medicinal properties and store them in a dry place.
Clean and dry the harvested rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 100°f for 2 hours to prevent spoilage.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a high-speed blender at 200 rpm for 2 minutes.
Mix 2 grams of the powder with 10 grams of vegetable cellulose or gelatin capsule filler in a glass container.
Fill 100-200 vegetarian capsules with the powder mixture using a powder filling machine or a manual capsule filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with nausea because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive system. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba can also help protect the stomach lining from damage, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause nausea. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba may help improve blood flow to the digestive system, which can aid in nutrient absorption and reduce nausea. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing nausea due to digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help reduce nausea by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that alleviate symptoms associated with digestive issues.
Terpenoids: Contribute to nausea relief by modulating gut motility and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Bilobalide: Assist in alleviating nausea by exerting a sedative effect on the central nervous system, thereby reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger nausea.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are commonly used to make capsules for nausea as they contain a compound called bilobalide, which has anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea effects.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree are also used in some herbal supplements for nausea, as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that can help alleviate nausea and other digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them completely in a warm place for 3 days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of ginkgo biloba powder with 10 grams of tapioca starch in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder-starch mixture using a capsule filler for 20 minutes.
Label and store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare capsules helps with nausea because of its active compound, anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system. The herb, also known as sweet fennel, is traditionally used to ease digestive discomfort, including nausea and indigestion. Fennel's anise-like flavor can help stimulate digestive enzymes and relieve bloating, reducing nausea and promoting a sense of calm. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate nausea and promote a healthy digestive system. It can be especially beneficial during pregnancy and chemotherapy.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: Acts as an antispasmodic and anxiolytic agent, helping to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce nausea caused by stress and anxiety.
Anethole: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe the digestive tract and alleviate nausea caused by inflammation and irritation.
Ferulic acid: Shows antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity, which helps to protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing nausea and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used to make capsules for nausea due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Fennel leaves are used to make capsules for nausea due to their anti-inflammatory and digestive-aiding properties.
Buds: Fennel buds are used to make capsules for nausea due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems at dawn when the dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested material in a warm place or using a dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind 10g of the dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 5g of vegetable cellulose to create a uniform blend for encapsulation.
Fill 50 capsules with the uniform blend and seal them immediately to preserve the potency of the herbal ingredient.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with nausea because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. This can help alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), stomach ulcers, and gastritis. The root's natural compounds, including glycyrrhizin, may also help regulate the body's response to stress, which can contribute to nausea and other digestive discomforts.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help with nausea by stabilizing the stomach lining and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to nausea and vomiting.
Flavonoids: They help with nausea by their antioxidant properties, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the stomach, helping to alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Glycyrrhizin: It helps with nausea by inhibiting the breakdown of stomach mucosa and promoting the repair of the stomach lining, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make capsules for nausea due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make capsules for nausea due to their flavonoid and saponin content, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe digestive issues.
Barks: The barks are used to make capsules for nausea due to their glycoside content, specifically glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Collect 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier or harvest them yourself.
Dry the roots further at 50°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content to 10% using a vacuum dryer.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 1000 rpm for 30 minutes.
Mix 500 grams of the powder with 500 grams of cellulose powder as the capsule filler.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine at 50 capsules per minute.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis capsules helps with nausea because of its unique alkaloid composition, particularly quinine and quinidine. These compounds have natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis capsules have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness and vertigo. By regulating the body's response to stimuli and calming the digestive system, Cinchona officinalis capsules can provide effective relief from nausea and related discomfort. This makes it a popular natural remedy for nausea and digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Quinine helps with nausea by reducing inflammation in the stomach and alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with gastrointestinal issues.
Cinchonidine: Cinchonidine helps with nausea by stimulating the central nervous system, which can help alleviate feelings of queasiness and dizziness.
Quinidine: Quinidine helps with nausea by reducing the excitability of the stomach muscles, thus preventing excessive contractions that can lead to nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make capsules for nausea due to their high content of quinine, which has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties that help alleviate nausea and other symptoms.
Barks: Used to make capsules for nausea due to their alkaloid content, particularly quinine, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea.
Rhyzomes: Used to make capsules for nausea due to their content of alkaloids like quinine, which helps to alleviate nausea and other symptoms by reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of dried cinchona officinalis bark in early morning to ensure highest alkaloid content.
Dry the harvested cinchona officinalis bark in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 1 part cinchona officinalis bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 part ground cinchona officinalis with 2 parts gelatin powder in a bowl for 3 minutes.
Fill gelatin-filled capsules with the cinchona officinalis mixture using a capsule filling machine for 2 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum capsules helps with nausea because they contain the bioactive compound piperine, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and promote digestion, alleviating nausea and discomfort. Additionally, piperine has been shown to have a sedative effect on the stomach, calming it and preventing the spasms that can lead to nausea. As a result, Piper nigrum capsules can provide quick relief from nausea and help restore digestive balance.
remedy_constituents: Piperine: Helps reduce nausea by inhibiting the serotonin receptors in the brain, which are responsible for nausea and vomiting.
Eugenol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate nausea caused by inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
Safrole: Has a sedative effect on the central nervous system, which can help reduce nausea by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Piper nigrum seeds are commonly used due to their high concentration of piperine, a compound that helps alleviate nausea.
Rhyzomes: Piper nigrum rhyzomes contain a high amount of piperine, which is used to treat nausea and other digestive issues.
Fruits: Piper nigrum fruits, also known as peppercorns, are used for their bioactive compounds that help in reducing nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Crush 20 grams of dried piper nigrum berries into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 10 grams of tapioca starch and 5 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Add 20 ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir until the powder is well combined for 5 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and seal 100 capsules for 30 minutes.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita capsules helps with nausea because they contain peppermint oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract and ease muscle spasms in the stomach. Peppermint oil also has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to nausea. By promoting digestion and relaxation, Mentha x piperita capsules provide relief from nausea and can help individuals feel more comfortable and alleviate discomfort associated with digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps to calm the stomach and relieve nausea by reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
Linalool: Linalool has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce nausea and vomiting by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol has antimicrobial properties that can help to alleviate nausea by reducing the growth of bacteria in the stomach and promoting a healthy gut microbiome.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide a cooling sensation and help relieve nausea.
Stems: Stems, especially the nodes, also contain essential oils that contribute to the plant's medicinal properties, including nausea relief.
Roots: The roots of the plant are rich in menthol and menthone, and are sometimes used as a substitute for leaves or in combination with them for added efficacy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning for maximum potency retention.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 30c for 2 hours using a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 15 minutes preparation time.
Mix 1g of the powdered plant material with 4g of vegetable cellulose and 1g of silicon dioxide for capsule filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the plant material mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa capsules helps with nausea because they contain the extract of oat, a natural anti-inflammatory agent. The compounds in Avena sativa, such as avenanthramides, have been shown to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to nausea. Additionally, Avena sativa has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, which can also help alleviate nausea and related symptoms. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support digestive health and alleviate nausea.
remedy_constituents: Avenacosides: These saponin glycosides help with nausea by reducing inflammation and modulating the gut-brain axis, leading to a calming effect on the digestive system.
Avenanthramides: These phenolic compounds help with nausea by inhibiting the release of inflammatory mediators and modulating the gut microbiome, resulting in reduced nausea and vomiting.
Sterols: These compounds, including beta-sitosterol, help with nausea by regulating gut motility and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, which alleviates nausea and vomiting symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Avena sativa are commonly used to make capsules for nausea due to their high concentration of fatty acids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
Stems: The stems of Avena sativa contain a significant amount of fiber, which can help in regulating bowel movements and alleviating nausea.
Leaves: The leaves of Avena sativa are used to make capsules for nausea due to their rich content of antioxidants and volatile oils that can help in calming the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh avena sativa roots and rhizomes in the morning after the dew has dried completely.
Clean and dry the harvested avena sativa roots and rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried avena sativa roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the avena sativa powder in 400 milligram dosages and seal the capsules immediately.
Store the avena sativa capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum capsules helps with nausea because they contain the herb milk thistle, which has been traditionally used to support liver health. The active compound silymarin in milk thistle helps to protect and regenerate liver cells, which can become damaged and contribute to nausea. By supporting liver function, Silybum marianum capsules may help to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with liver disease, as well as other conditions such as chemotherapy-induced nausea and certain medications. This can lead to improved overall well-being and quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate nausea.
Silymarin: It acts as an anti-emetic by protecting the stomach lining from damage and reducing inflammation, thereby alleviating nausea and vomiting.
Icariin: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate nausea, and also has a protective effect on the stomach lining.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make capsules due to their high concentration of silymarin, a compound that helps alleviate nausea and other gastrointestinal issues.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules as they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce nausea and vomiting.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help alleviate nausea and other gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum seeds in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe and dry.
Clean and dry the seeds in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered seeds with a small amount of gelatin or vegetarian capsule filler in a ratio of 10:1.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the powdered seed mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum capsules helps with nausea because they contain a potent compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This compound helps to soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea by reducing inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract. Additionally, Cinnamomum verum has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to regulate digestive function and alleviate nausea associated with stress, anxiety, and motion sickness. Its natural properties make it a popular remedy for nausea relief.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: It helps alleviate nausea by inhibiting the release of gastric acid and reducing inflammation in the stomach.
Cinnamic acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory compounds.
Linalool: It has a sedative effect, which can help calm the stomach and reduce nausea by regulating the body's digestive processes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea properties.
Barks: Used due to their essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea properties, as well as their ability to warm the body and relieve digestive issues.
Stems: Used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate nausea, as well as their antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered cinnamomum verum with 10 grams of cellulose or gelatin to form a uniform blend.
Add 5 grams of tapioca starch to the mixture and blend until well combined for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cinnamomum verum mixture using a capsule filler or spoon.
Store the herbal cinnamomum verum capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.
